title: identity — authorization policies
description: Only ONE policy exists: `IdentityPolicy`. Every ability is gated behind the `platform_admin` guard — the tenant-audience `sanctum` guard NEVER interacts with Identity rows directly. Tenant callers reach Identity substrate only via auth-service actions (login, MFA challenge, password reset) which route through domain-layer authorization, not this policy.

abilities.scope: Platform ops. Redacted list (email hash-fingerprint + metadata; no PII).
Platform ops. Redacted detail. See `viewPii` for the PII unmute.
Platform-security only. Unmutes email + dob on the response. Never unmutes regulated_secret fields. Every read of a PII-unmuted response writes an audit entry keyed to the actor.
Platform support. Clears `locked_until` + resets `failed_attempts_count`. Fires IdentityUnlocked(trigger=admin_action).
Platform-security. Sets a system-generated ephemeral password + revokes all sessions + emails a reset link. Requires justification (audit-logged).
Platform-security only. Regenerates the KMS-wrapped MFA secret without exposing plaintext. Used during KMS key rotation events.
Compliance officer only. Soft-deletes the Identity (starts the GDPR hold-period). Actual hard delete is automated via PurgeSoftDeletedIdentitiesJob.
Compliance officer only. Reverses an in-flight soft-delete (before the hold-period elapsed). Cannot restore a hard-deleted row.

boundary_note: This policy applies EXCLUSIVELY to the `platform_admin` guard. Under the `sanctum` guard, the Identity row is never a first-class resource — tenant callers cannot list, show, or mutate Identity directly. Any tenant-audience code that receives an authorization refusal here has taken a wrong path; it should be routed through `identity/auth` actions instead. Guard boundary is enforced at the auth-service action layer, not by this policy.
